← All worlds/World 14
World 14 · 2 states

Burnout

Films for the person who kept going long after the tank was empty — the particular silence of having given too much for too long.

Choose an emotion
Empty AchievementCompassion Fatigue
On Compassion Fatigue

You have been the strong one. The one who stayed. The one who showed up when everyone else found reasons not to. You held other people’s pain for so long it became indistinguishable from your own. And now something in you has gone quiet — not peaceful quiet, but numb quiet. You still care. You just cannot find the caring right now. These films understand the cost of sustained empathy. They do not shame the caregiver for reaching a limit. They witness the particular exhaustion of those who made their love a daily practice.

Compassion Fatigue

Films for when there is nothing left to give.

10Curated films

10 films — quick browse

CS8.9

Ordinary People

Robert Redford · 1980

🏆 Won 4 Academy Awards incl. Best Picture

CS9.3

Manchester by the Sea

Kenneth Lonergan · 2016

🏆 Won 2 Academy Awards

CS9.0

The Farewell

Lulu Wang · 2019

CS8.5

Still Alice

Richard Glatzer & Wash Westmoreland · 2014

🏆 Won Academy Award Best Actress

CS9.0

Wit

Mike Nichols · 2001

CS8.4

The Savages

Tamara Jenkins · 2007

CS8.6

Maria Full of Grace

CS8.8

A Marriage Story

CS8.7

In America

Jim Sheridan · 2002

CS8.9
1980

Ordinary People

Robert Redford

View Film →

A family struggles to survive after a boating accident kills one son and leaves the other drowning in guilt — a film about the enormous effort of ordinary living after catastrophe.

CS9.3
2016

Manchester by the Sea

Kenneth Lonergan

View Film →

A handyman in Boston returns to his small hometown after his brother's death to become guardian of his teenage nephew — carrying a grief so large it has hollowed him out.

CS9.0
2019

The Farewell

Lulu Wang

View Film →

A Chinese-American woman returns to China for a fake family wedding — really an orchestrated final visit to a grandmother kept unaware of her terminal diagnosis.

CS8.5
2014

Still Alice

Richard Glatzer & Wash Westmoreland

View Film →

A linguistics professor is diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's and the film documents her deterioration and her family's attempts to hold onto her.

CS9.0
2001

Wit

Mike Nichols

View Film →

A brilliant, acerbic English literature professor faces ovarian cancer — and finds that the intellectual defenses she has built cannot protect her from the need for simple human comfort.

CS8.4
2007

The Savages

Tamara Jenkins

View Film →

Two adult siblings must confront their mutual failure to care for a difficult, estranged father who is slipping into dementia.

CS8.6
2004

Maria Full of Grace

Joshua Marston

View Film →

A Colombian teenager, exhausted by the dead-end of her life, agrees to work as a drug mule — and the film follows her across a journey that tests everything she has.

CS8.8
2006

Little Children

Todd Field

View Film →

Two unhappily married parents in suburban Massachusetts begin an affair while their community's attention is consumed by a registered sex offender who has returned to the neighborhood.

CS9.4
1973

A Marriage Story

Ingmar Bergman

View Film →

Six episodes document a decade of a Swedish couple's marriage, its deterioration, their divorce, and the strange love that persists through the wreckage.

CS8.7
2002

In America

Jim Sheridan

View Film →

An Irish immigrant family arrives in New York City carrying the grief of a lost child — and the children's resilience and openness slowly pull the grieving adults back toward life.

For the slow rebuild after the wound.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.
